bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility to establish a strong anchor. I am targeting my most preferred values across all issues, particularly on my highest-weighted issues F, E, and B, while I leave room for future concessions on my lower-weighted issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent's first offer matches my top preference for issueE (valueA). To show a willingness to compromise without giving up significant utility this early, I will concede completely on issueD (which has zero weight for me) by offering the opponent's preferred valueA, while maintaining my top preferences for all other issues.

In the early stage of this negotiation (`t=0.04`), I need to maintain high overall utility while making minor concessions to show flexibility and discover the opponent's priorities. The opponent has requested `valueA` for `issueA` and `valueB` for `issueD`, both of which are relatively inexpensive for me to concede. I will accept those values for `issueA` and `issueD` while holding firm on my most valuable assets (`issueB`, `issueE`, `issueF`), keeping my expected utility high.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.06). We agree on issueD and issueE, where the opponent has accepted my highest utility values, so I will maintain those in my bid. I am also currently matching the opponent's demand on issueA. To make further progress, I will make a slight concession on issueC (from valueC to valueB) while firmly holding my highest priority issues (issueB and issueF).

The current time fraction is very early in the negotiation. We have already found common ground on issues D and E, and the opponent has consistently requested 'valueA' for issues A and C. I am willing to concede immediately on issue C to match their demand in order to show cooperation, while holding firm on my most highly valued issues B and F.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.10). The opponent has repeatedly proposed the exact same offer in their last four turns. Since my last offers made several concessions on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueD, issueC), and the opponent hasn't reciprocated on my high-weight issues (issueB and issueF), I will hold firm on these critical issues while slightly varying my offer on issueC to avoid identical repetition and signal a need for their concession.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.12). The opponent has been repeating the same offer while we have explored concessions on our side for issues A, C, and D. Since the opponent appears rigid, I will reiterate an offer that gives them what they asked for on issues A, D, and E (where our preferences align or the cost of concession is low) while holding strictly firm on our high-importance issues B, C, and F to signal our core priorities and invite them to concede.

The negotiation is still early at t=0.14. The opponent is stubbornly repeating an offer that provides low utility on my primary issues (issueF and issueB), but they have conceded issueE and issueD to my preferred options (or values that work for me). To show flexibility while protecting my highest weight issues, I will propose a new mix: conceding issueC to their preferred valueA, but asking back for my preferred valueC on issueA while keeping issueB, issueE, and issueF at my most favorable values.

The negotiation is in the early stages (t=0.16). The opponent has rigidly repeated the exact same offer (yielding me a very low utility of ~0.42), so I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueF and issueB) as well as the agreed-upon issueE. I will present a slight variation in my lower-priority issues (issueA and issueC) to see if we can find a breakthrough, aiming for a utility of around 0.85 while exploring the search space.

The opponent is repeating the same offer since t=0.04, which provides a total utility of around 0.41, vastly below what I can accept this early in the negotiation. I will maintain my top values on my highest-weighted issues (issueF and issueB) while matching the opponent's requests on issueA, issueD, and issueE. I am slightly adjusting issueC to probe for any flexibility.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.20), but the opponent has held firm on the exact same offer since the beginning. Since we already align perfectly on issueD and issueE, I will maintain our mutually preferred values there. However, to signal that they must reciprocate concessions on the higher-weight issues (issueB, issueF), I am retreating closer to my maximum utility configuration.

The opponent has been making the exact same bid repeatedly, showing no willingness to concede. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueF, issueB) and the shared issueE where we both prefer valueA. I am offering a slightly different combination on my lower-weighted issues (issueA and issueC) to see if we can find mutual ground.

The opponent continues to repeat the exact same offer, showing no indication of flexibility so early in the negotiation (t=0.24). Their overall offer provides very low utility to me because it demands large concessions on my most important issues (F and B). I will reciprocate their rigid behavior by maintaining my stance on F and B, while continuing to align on their top choices for D and E where we share mutual benefit, and offering a moderate concession on issue A to encourage movement.

The opponent continues to repeat the exact same offer, which includes my preferred choices for issueD (valueB) and issueE (valueA). They clearly value `issueB: valueB`, so I will systematically concede on `issueB` (from valueC to valueB) while resetting my position on `issueA` and `issueC` to my most preferred values. This will communicate a willingness to find a compromise on `issueB` without dropping my overall utility too low.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same stubborn offer since the early stages. I will attempt to break the deadlock by yielding completely to their repeated choices on issueA and issueB, as well as maintaining their favored values on issueD and issueE. I must hold firm on issueC and issueF to keep my utility well above the reservation threshold.

The opponent is rigidly repeating the same offer (`issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueA`) which yields a utility of ~0.41 to me. Since we are at `t=0.3` (early/mid-game), I need to hold firm on my highest-priority issue (`issueF`) but I can explore giving them their requested values on some moderately important issues like `issueB` and `issueC` to see if they are willing to step away from their repetitive offer. I will propose a tradeoff that matches their preferences on `issueB`, `issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueE` while keeping my strongest preferences on `issueF` and `issueA`.

The opponent is repeating the exact same offer, which is aligned with my interests on issues D and E, but conflicts significantly on my highest priority issue F. To find a workable compromise without conceding my core interests at `t=0.32`, I will offer their exact preferred values on issues A, B, D, and E. This gives them what they want on four out of six issues, while I retain my highest utility value on issue F and a moderate value on issue C, targeting an overall utility around 0.68.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their exact offer from t=0.04 onwards, showing no concessions on their preferred values for A, B, C, and F. We both agree on issue E (valueA) and issue D (valueB), which is mutually beneficial. To maximize my utility while showing minor flexibility without needlessly surrendering my heavily weighted issues (F and B), I will propose a slight concession on issue A, maintaining my core utility drivers.

The opponent continues to repeat the identical offer requesting valueA for issues A, C, E, and F, and valueB for issues B and D. Since the time fraction is 0.36, I will maintain my positions on high-importance issues (issueF and issueE) while exploring new mid-range utility trade-offs, particularly by partially accommodating their request on issueB and issueC. Their rigid behavior indicates they may have stringent preferences, but I must secure a utility around 0.72 at this stage to avoid conceding too early.

I will maintain my core high-utility issues but attempt a compromise on issueF, which the opponent seems to desire as valueA, by stepping down to valueB. This offer preserves an overall utility of approximately 0.68 for me while conceding on issueA, issueD, and issueE to the values the opponent has consistently demanded. Time is at 0.38, so finding middle ground is appropriate without crashing to my minimum bounds.

At t=0.4, the opponent has held completely firm on their offer for many rounds. Fortunately, we naturally agree on issueD and issueE. In order to break the deadlock and show substantial compromise, I will concede entirely to their demands on issueA, issueB, and issueC, which are of moderate or low importance to me. I will only hold firm on my highest-weight priority, issueF, maintaining a reasonable total utility of roughly 0.65 while hopefully eliciting a mutual concession.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that provides me with moderate utility, mostly due to shared preferences on issueE and issueD. Since we are approaching the mid-game (t=0.42), I will concede on issueB to match their preference (`valueB`) and try issuing `valueA` for issueD, which they offered in their very first bid. Meanwhile, I will remain firm on my highest-weight issue (`issueF` = `valueC`), signaling I am willing to meet their demands on lesser-weighted components if they respect my critical needs.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer since the beginning of the negotiation involving high pressure on several issues. Since we are approaching the halfway mark (t=0.44), I need to discover whether they are willing to accept compromises on my highest weighted issue (issueF) or if they are just anchoring. I will concede issueF to 'valueB' to see if a middle value on my most important issue helps break the deadlock, while keeping my most preferred values on other issues to maintain an overall utility above 0.7.

The opponent continues to repeat their bid exactly, insisting on their preferred values for issues A, B, C, and F. Since we are approaching the midway point (t=0.46), I will make a strategic concession on my most valuable issue, issueF, reducing it to valueB to see if this breaks the deadlock. However, to maintain a reasonable utility (0.60), I will request my top value for issueB in exchange, while giving the opponent their exact requested values on issues A, C, D, and E.

The opponent is holding firm on their exact same bid since t=0.04, which yields me a low utility of 0.416. Since we are around the mid-game (t=0.48), I need to continue testing different trade-offs without dropping my utility too drastically. I will align with their preference on issueE and issueD where our interests match, and partially concede on issueF (from valueC to valueB) while keeping issueA and issueB high for myself, targeting an overall utility around 0.66 to find where they might be flexible.

The negotiation is at the midpoint (t=0.50). The opponent has been repeating the exact same offer, demonstrating uncompromising behavior on all issues. We share a mutual interest in `issueE: valueA` and `issueD: valueB`, so I will continue giving them those. I will explore a new combination by compromising to `valueB` on `issueA` and `issueC`, while maintaining my most important points (`issueF` and `issueB`) at my highest utility to keep my target utility above 0.75.

The opponent has stubbornly continued proposing the identical offer since t=0.04, heavily demanding `issueF: valueA` and `issueB: valueB` alongside other values. Since we are halfway to the deadline (t=0.52), I will explore what happens if I concede on their rigid demand for `issueF: valueA`, which drops my utility on that issue but I will compensate by claiming my top values on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`. This package retains a solid 0.66 utility for me while giving the opponent both their exact requested items on D, E, and crucially F, to see if F is their primary driver.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer since t=0.04 (`issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueA`). I have previously tried matching them completely on every issue except `issueF` at t=0.40, which they rejected, suggesting they heavily favor `issueF: valueA` or `issueC: valueA`. However, my utility for conceding everything they want is extremely low (~0.41), so I must find a compromise. I will offer a combination matching them fully on issues A, B, D, and E, whilst trying to secure my higher preferences on issues F and C to maintain an acceptable utility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueA) since early in the negotiation, which yields very low utility for me (0.416) primarily due to giving me my worst outcome on my highest-weight issue, issueF. It is midpoint in the negotiation, so I will propose a compromise: accepting the opponent's preferred values for issues A, B, D, and E (where we share interest), while making a partial concession on issueF to valueB and holding onto valueC for issueC to keep my utility reasonable (0.553).

The opponent is rigidly repeating the same offer (`A: valueA, B: valueB, C: valueA, D: valueB, E: valueA, F: valueA`). Their offer gives me very low utility (around 0.41), heavily penalizing my most valued issues (F and B). To encourage compromise while safeguarding my utility, I will accommodate their preference for `issueA: valueA`, `issueD: valueB`, and `issueE: valueA`, while exploring a new valid tradeoff on `issueB` (proposing `valueA`) and remaining firm on my highest-weight issues `issueC` and `issueF`.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer across numerous rounds, signaling a high reservation value or a stubborn strategy. Although I am willing to find middle ground, we are still at t=0.6, giving us time to negotiate. I will offer a package that captures high utility for me on my most important issues (issueF, issueE) while conceding on issueB and matching on issueD and issueE.

The opponent is repeating the exact same offer (`issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, issueE: valueA, issueF: valueA`), showing zero willingness to concede so far. Since `issueF` is my most highly weighted issue, I cannot yield entirely on it without destroying my utility. I am willing to concede `issueA`, `issueB`, `issueD`, and `issueE` to their exact requested values, but I will hold firm on `issueF` and `issueC` to maintain a utility within the acceptable >0.70 range at t=0.62.

The opponent's most recent offer diverges slightly from their typical pattern by changing `issueA` and `issueD`, though their change on `issueA` yields worse utility for me. To gently move towards an agreement as we cross the `t=0.6` mark, I will accommodate their newest preference for `issueD: valueA`, maintain their preferred values for `issueA` and `issueB` (which both provide me decent utility), and make a slight concession on `issueC` down to `valueB`, while holding firm on my most important issue `issueF`.

I am prioritizing my highest-weight issues (issueF and issueE) while observing the opponent's consistency on issueB (valueB), issueD (valueB), and issueE (valueA). Since issueE=valueA is mutually beneficial, I will hold firm on it, and I will also hold firm on my most important issue, issueF (valueC). I'll concede on issueB, issueD, and their historically preferred issueA, while attempting to extract some utility on issueC to counterbalance the concessions.

The opponent has recently shown a preference for `issueD: valueA` and still insists on `issueB: valueB` and `issueC: valueA` with minor shifts in `issueA`. Since we are at `t=0.68`, I will make a concession by providing their preferred value for `issueD` and `issueE`, while partially yielding on `issueB` and `issueC`. I will stand firm on my most important issue, `issueF`, as well as `issueA` to maintain a viable utility of approximately 0.71.

The opponent has shown consistent preference for issueB=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueA, with a recent shift towards issueA=valueB and issueD=valueA in their offers around t=0.64-0.68. Since issueE=valueA is already my top preference, I will align with their recent requests for issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, and issueD=valueA. However, issueF is my most critical issue (weight 0.3), so I will hold firm on issueF=valueC to ensure a satisfactory baseline utility while finding common ground.

The opponent is consistently offering a combination that includes `issueE: valueA` and `issueD: valueB`, which happen to align well with my preferences or have zero weight for me. However, they continue to demand `issueF: valueA` and `issueB: valueB`. Given that the deadline is approaching (t=0.72), I will concede further by adopting their preferred values for issues A, B, and E, while also incorporating `issueD: valueA` which they've requested in recent rounds. I'll maintain my firm stance on `issueF: valueC`, which brings my targeted utility to roughly 0.68.

The opponent has rigidly insisted on a specific combination of values, particularly `issueC: valueA`, `issueF: valueA`, and `issueE: valueA`. Because `issueF` has my highest weight (0.30) and `issueF: valueC` is my top value, I will hold firm on it while yielding completely to their preferences on `issueC` and `issueA` to test if they are willing to concede on their remaining unmet demand. This maintains a utility of around 0.65 for me while providing them nearly their entire requested package.

The opponent seems very steadfast, repeatedly aiming for valueA on most issues and valueB for issueB. However, in recent offers, they have alternated to valueB for issueA and valueA for issueD. Since my weights for issueA, C, and D are low, I will offer a combination fully matching their last alternating offer on these lower-weight issues (issueA: valueB, issueD: valueA) and match issueB, C, and E. I will stand entirely firm on my highest-weight issueF (valueC) to maintain an expected utility of around 0.61, offering a significant concession while preserving a fair payout for myself.

The negotiation is entering the late stage at t=0.78. The opponent has been highly rigid, especially insisting on issueB=valueB, issueC=valueA, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueA. Since issueE=valueA aligns perfectly with my highest utility for that issue, I will continue proposing it. To find common ground, I will concede almost entirely to their recent configuration but present a compromise on issueF (valueB instead of valueA) and issueC (valueB instead of valueA) to keep the offer above my reservation threshold and stimulate a deal.

The negotiation is entering its late stage (t=0.8). The opponent has shown steadfastness on several issues (B=valueB, C=valueA, E=valueA) but oscillates on issueA (valueA/valueB) and issueD (valueA/valueB). I will concede to their most frequent preferences on issues B, C, and E, as well as choosing combinations of A and D they have proposed. However, I will hold firm on issueF (valueC), which carries my highest weight, hoping the concessions on all other domains will secure their agreement.

The opponent has finally shown concessions on issueF (moving from valueA to valueB), signaling flexibility. I will attempt to lock in their preferred values for most other issues (issueA, issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE) where our alignment is close or my weights are relatively low. I will hold firm on issueF (valueC) since it carries my highest aggregate weight, yielding a solid utility while moving closer to an agreement before the deadline.

The opponent's recent offers show a willingness to deviate from their initial firm position on issueA, issueD, and importantly, issueF. Since issueF is my highest-weighted open issue, I will stubbornly hold onto valueC for issueF. However, to entice the opponent, I am thoroughly conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueC to align with their demands. This targets a respectable utility of 0.61 for me while demonstrating significant flexibility.

The opponent is consistently demanding `issueB: valueB` and `issueC: valueA`, while showing some flexibility by occasionally offering `issueF: valueB` instead of their preferred `valueA`. I will continue to fully concede on their likely most important issues (`issueB`, `issueC`, `issueE`) and offer varying combinations for `issueA` and `issueD` to explore acceptable trade-offs. I am holding firm on `issueF: valueC` since it carries my highest weight, hoping this favorable package across all other issues will persuade them.
